message:
🌱 Add traefik: Allow to add path prefix for both api and dashboard mission (#1588)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "traefik-5853-allow-to-add-path-prefix-for-both-api-and-dashboard-endpoints",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "traefik: Allow to add path prefix for both api and dashboard endpoints",
+    "description": "Allow to add path prefix for both api and dashboard endpoints. This issue affects 120+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify traefik troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your traefik deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik -n traefik --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review traefik configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant traefik configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik
kubectl get configmap -n traefik -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=traefik
```
### Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?

Feature

### What did you expect to see?

As #5374 has been fixed, now dashboard assets can be served under any path prefix, but it continues to fetch api data from `/api`, which seems to be"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Allow to add path prefix for both api and dashboard…",
+        "description": "On traefik:v2.1.1 this use case is working for me. This is how it looks like:

`compose-traefik.yml`

```yml
    labels:
        #___ core configs
      - traefik.enable=true
        #___ set traefik dashboard + API. The API is available at: mydomain.club/api/http/routers
      -
```yaml
apiVersion: extensions/v1beta1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: traefik-ingress
  namespace: kube-system
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: traefik
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/rule-type: PathPrefixStrip
spec:
  rules:
  - host: host.traefik.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /traefik
        backend:
          serviceName: traefik-dashboard
          servicePort: 80
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Allow to add path prefix for both api and… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=traefik -n traefik --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n traefik --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "On traefik:v2.1.1 this use case is working for me. This is how it looks like:

`compose-traefik.yml`

```yml
    labels:
        #___ core configs
      - traefik.enable=true
        #___ set traefik dashboard + API.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "apiVersion: extensions/v1beta1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: traefik-ingress
  namespace: kube-system
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/ingress.class: traefik
    traefik.ingress.kubernetes.io/rule-type: PathPrefixStrip
spec:
  rules:
  - host: host.traefik.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /traefik
        backend:
          serviceName: traefik-dashboard
          servicePort: 80",
+        "labels:
        #___ core configs
      - traefik.enable=true
        #___ set traefik dashboard + API. The API is available at: mydomain.club/api/http/routers
      - "traefik.http.routers.traefik.rule=Host(`mydomain.club`) && (PathPrefix(`/traefik`) || PathPrefix(`/api`))"
      - "traefik.http.routers.traefik.service=api@internal"
        #___ set TLS (https)
      - "traefik.http.routers.traefik.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.traefik.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.traefik.tls.certresolver=leresolver"
        #___ use these middlewares
      - "traefik.http.routers.traefik.middlewares=traefik-strip"
        #___ specific middleware for traefik (it enables the dashboard at mydomain.com/traefik)
      - "traefik.http.middlewares.traefik-strip.stripprefix.pre"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "traefik",
+      "community",
+      "ingress",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "traefik"
+    ],

+      "troubleshoot"

+      "repo": "https://github.com/traefik/traefik"
+    },

+    "comments": 74,

+  },
